excerpt: Mixed-status deaths in 1982 are given as roughly 17,000-19,000.
transform_note: Used only as mixed-status context; not separable enough for core DR.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence that this is a real broad-order total, low confidence for direct DR use.

excerpt: PalQuest summarizes a pre-war PLO fighter estimate of 15,000-18,000 in Lebanon.
transform_note: Denominator context only; not a theater-specific person-month series.
confidence_note: Useful ceiling/floor context but too broad for a single-episode denominator.

excerpt: PalQuest reports roughly 76,000 Israeli troops in the initial invasion force.
transform_note: Entry-force figure only; not a person-month estimate and not valid for the whole heterogeneous 1982 war.
confidence_note: High confidence for the invasion-force order of magnitude.

excerpt: PalQuest gives 376 Israeli soldiers killed from June to September 1982.
transform_note: Attacker identity is mixed across adversaries, so inflicting_side is left null.
confidence_note: Good for Israeli military deaths, poor for episode-level DR because the episode is too broad.

excerpt: HRW summary: some 120 Lebanese civilians were killed during Operation Accountability.
transform_note: Used as the main best estimate for Lebanese civilian direct deaths.
confidence_note: High confidence for a civilian point estimate in the 118-120 range.

excerpt: Prime Minister Hariri's split gives 118 civilian deaths.
transform_note: Used as the conservative low civilian-death anchor.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence; official Lebanese split but still official-source dependent.

excerpt: Hariri's split implies nine Lebanon-side organized armed personnel killed.
transform_note: Combines one Lebanese soldier and eight Hezbollah fighters.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence; likely a lower bound.

excerpt: Alternative mixed-status total: 147 Lebanese killed, vast majority civilians.
transform_note: Excluded from core status-specific estimates because the civilian/combatant split is not explicit.

excerpt: HRW reports roughly 300,000 displaced in Lebanon, plus 50,000 refugees leaving camps elsewhere in the report.
transform_note: Denominator context only; insufficient to reconstruct daily civilian person-month exposure.
confidence_note: High confidence that displacement was very large.

excerpt: Israeli military officer estimate: about 20 Hezbollah killed.
transform_note: Used as the middle anchor for Lebanon-side combatant deaths.

excerpt: IDF retrospective page gives 50 'terrorists' killed.
transform_note: Used as the upper bound for combatant deaths; terminology kept source-native.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence; likely an upper official claim.

excerpt: IDF page gives two Israeli civilians killed during the operation.
transform_note: Used for Hezbollah-inflicted civilian deaths on the Israeli side.

excerpt: IDF page gives one IDF fatality during the operation.
transform_note: Used for Hezbollah-inflicted Israeli military deaths.

excerpt: Amnesty reports Lebanese military records showing 154 civilian deaths.
transform_note: Core civilian death count for the 1996 episode.
confidence_note: High confidence for the 154 figure; a separate Amnesty summary says 'over 150'.

excerpt: Amnesty reports five military personnel killed in Lebanon.
transform_note: These are non-Hezbollah organized armed personnel and are added to Hezbollah fatality claims when building Lebanon-side military estimates.

excerpt: Amnesty reports 351 civilian injuries in Lebanon.
transform_note: Injuries kept separate from deaths.

excerpt: Amnesty reports 11 military personnel wounded in Lebanon.
transform_note: Injuries kept separate from deaths.

excerpt: Hezbollah's own claim for Hezbollah fatalities is 14.
transform_note: Used as the lower Hezbollah-fatality anchor.
confidence_note: Low-to-moderate confidence because it is a self-report and may undercount.

excerpt: Israeli claim for Hezbollah fatalities is 50.
transform_note: Used as the upper Hezbollah-fatality anchor.
confidence_note: Low-to-moderate confidence because it is a belligerent claim.

excerpt: Amnesty reports over 300,000 Lebanese displaced.
transform_note: Denominator context only.
confidence_note: High confidence that displacement was massive.

excerpt: Amnesty reports 30,000 Israelis displaced.
transform_note: Denominator context only.

excerpt: Amnesty reports 62 Israeli civilians wounded.
transform_note: Injuries kept separate from deaths.

excerpt: Amnesty reports two Israeli soldiers wounded.
transform_note: Injuries kept separate from deaths.

excerpt: Amnesty states no Israelis were killed during the 17-day operation.
transform_note: Used as context; not separated into civilian and military zero claims in the core bundle.
confidence_note: High confidence for zero total Israeli deaths in this episode.

excerpt: HRW accounted for 1,109 direct deaths in Lebanon during the 34-day war.
transform_note: Mixed-status total used alongside the explicit civilian/combatant split.
confidence_note: High confidence for HRW's accounted total; not necessarily identical to final official total.

excerpt: HRW's best-known Lebanese civilian death split is approximately 860 civilians.
transform_note: Primary civilian numerator for Israeli-inflicted Lebanese deaths.
confidence_note: High confidence relative to other open sources.

excerpt: HRW's explicit combatant count is approximately 250.
transform_note: Primary low/best anchor for Lebanon-side military deaths.
confidence_note: High confidence as a documented lower-bound-style combatant count.

excerpt: UNSG reports official Lebanese total deaths as 1,187 as of 31 August 2006.
transform_note: Used as a broader official total, not as a status-split count.
confidence_note: High confidence for the official aggregate.

excerpt: UNSG reports 4,092 injured in Lebanon.
transform_note: Injury context only; no clean civilian/military split.

excerpt: UNSG gives an estimate of about 1 million Lebanese displaced.
transform_note: Major input into civilian person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: High confidence for order of magnitude.

excerpt: UNHRC COI states that nearly all 220,000 inhabitants of Dahiyeh evacuated.
transform_note: Used to justify reducing Lebanese civilian person-month exposure below a static full-population count.
confidence_note: High confidence for Dahiyeh evacuation scale.

excerpt: UNHRC COI says the fighting was between Israel and Hezbollah; the LAF did not actively participate.
transform_note: Used to define the military victim side in Lebanese combatant estimates.

excerpt: RAND summarizes a broader civilian death range of 900-1,100.
transform_note: Used only to widen the upper interval; HRW remains the best point estimate.

excerpt: RAND summarizes Hezbollah deaths as ranging from 250 to 800.
transform_note: Used to broaden the military fatality interval beyond HRW's lower documented count.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence as a secondary synthesis of disagreement.

excerpt: RAND reports an Israeli estimate of roughly 10,000 Hezbollah fighters.
transform_note: Used as the broad-force upper bound for Hezbollah combatant exposure.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence; likely includes forces beyond the most intense battle area.

geographic_scope: South of the Litani River, Lebanon
excerpt: CSI study reports 3,000 Hezbollah fighters south of the Litani.
transform_note: Used as the low-force denominator scenario.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence for the south-Litani battle space.

excerpt: JBI gives the broad exposed civilian population in northern Israel as about 1.5 million.
transform_note: Upper anchor for Israeli civilian person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: High confidence for order of magnitude.

excerpt: JBI reports around 330,000 displaced from northern Israel.
transform_note: Used to avoid assuming the full 1.5 million remained present in the strike zone at all times.

excerpt: JBI reports about 1 million in shelters in northern Israel.
transform_note: Supports a lower average exposed population no smaller than about 1 million.

excerpt: Home-front-only Israeli deaths inside Israel: 43 civilians and 12 soldiers.
transform_note: Used to show that home-front military deaths are lower than total war military deaths.

excerpt: HRW home-front study gives 43 Israeli civilian deaths.
transform_note: Consistent with the UNSG report.

excerpt: UNSG reports 117 Israeli soldiers killed in the war.
transform_note: Used for the episode-wide Israeli military death numerator.

excerpt: CSI study reports 10,000 IDF troops in southern Lebanon by Aug. 5.
transform_note: Used as the floor operational anchor for directly assigned IDF forces.

excerpt: RAND gives a 30,000-troop operating figure in southern Lebanon.
transform_note: Used as the best IDF directly assigned force scenario.

excerpt: RAND notes 62,000 reservists activated during the war.
transform_note: Used as an upper ceiling for directly assigned IDF exposure, not as a point estimate of soldiers physically in Lebanon at all times.

excerpt: INSS edited volume says the IDF mobilized close to 50,000 soldiers.
transform_note: Used as cross-check that the directly assigned force likely exceeded the 10,000 in-Lebanon figure.

excerpt: HRW country chapter cites more than 3,961 people killed in Lebanon by Israeli strikes before the 2024 ceasefire.
transform_note: Aggregate death claim only; no full civilian/combatant split.
confidence_note: High confidence for the lower-bound aggregate; not usable as a clean DR numerator.

excerpt: HRW cites 736 women among those killed in Lebanon.
transform_note: Auxiliary subgroup claim only; not a substitute for civilian totals.
confidence_note: High confidence that the subgroup is source-native, but not a clean civilian-status series.

excerpt: HRW cites 248 children among the Lebanese dead.
transform_note: Auxiliary subgroup claim only.
confidence_note: High confidence that the figure is source-native.

excerpt: HRW gives more than 1.2 million displaced by the November 2024 ceasefire.
transform_note: Important denominator context, but the footprint changed too much for a single person-month series.

geographic_scope: Northern Israel and occupied Golan Heights
excerpt: HRW gives at least 30 civilians killed on the Israeli/Golan side by Hezbollah munitions.
transform_note: Lower-bound civilian numerator only; not matched by a reliable Israeli military-fatality and denominator package.

excerpt: ACLED explicitly says the militant/civilian breakdown is unclear.
transform_note: This is the main reason the 2023-2024 episode is not fully estimated.

geographic_scope: Bint Jubayl, Marjayun, and Tyre districts
excerpt: ACLED identifies Bint Jubayl, Marjayun, and Tyre as the main districts of the ground operation.
transform_note: Useful for geometry notes only.

excerpt: Reuters reports 3,768 people killed in Lebanon by the ceasefire date.
transform_note: Cross-check on the aggregate scale; not status-clean.

excerpt: Reuters reports roughly 1.4 million uprooted by the conflict in Lebanon.
transform_note: Cross-check on displacement scale.

excerpt: Reuters reports around 60,000 Israelis evacuated from northern border communities.
transform_note: Useful for the Israeli-side exposure footprint but insufficient for a full denominator series.

excerpt: Reuters March 6 report places the renewed-war start on Monday, 2026-03-02.
transform_note: Used to set the episode start date.

excerpt: Reuters March 6 gives 123 killed and 683 wounded, explicitly without civilian/combatant separation.
transform_note: Not used for DR because status separation is absent.
confidence_note: High confidence for aggregate reporting at that time.

excerpt: Reuters March 7 updates the death toll to nearly 300 and notes mass displacement.
transform_note: Aggregate context only.

excerpt: Reuters March 8 gives 394 killed in Lebanon by the research cut-off.
transform_note: Aggregate only; the ministry does not otherwise distinguish civilians and military personnel.
confidence_note: High confidence for the reported total.

excerpt: Reuters March 8 cites at least 83 children among the Lebanese dead.
transform_note: Auxiliary subgroup claim only.
confidence_note: High confidence as source-native subgroup count.

excerpt: Reuters March 8 cites 42 women among the Lebanese dead.
transform_note: Auxiliary subgroup claim only.
confidence_note: High confidence as source-native subgroup count.

excerpt: Israeli military claim: about 200 Hezbollah militants killed by March 8.
transform_note: Belligerent claim only; not independently reconciled with total deaths.

excerpt: Reuters March 8 reports two Israeli soldiers killed in southern Lebanon.
transform_note: Too early and incomplete for a full Israeli-side death series.

excerpt: Reuters explicitly notes that the Lebanese ministry does not separate civilians from military personnel.
transform_note: This blocks a defensible DR at the research cut-off.

excerpt: Amnesty summary states over 150 civilians were killed, all by Israeli attacks.
transform_note: Used as a floor consistent with the 154-civilian record figure.
confidence_note: High confidence as a lower-bound statement.
